content.claim: Early school-leaving is concentrated among Roma (58.7% of Roma 18-24-year-olds are school dropouts versus 9.3% non-Roma) and rural students; only 12.1% of disadvantaged students reach good level in basic skills versus EU average 16.3%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: System-wide outcome gaps do not isolate causal contribution of 6/8-year gimnázium specifically
Early leaving concentrates mainly in vocational tracks rather than as direct consequence of gimnázium non-admission
Does not distinguish mechanism-specific effects from broader structural tracking
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
